title: Improvements in or relating to microsphere-enhanced spectroscopy
abstract: An apparatus (100) for carrying out microsphere-enhanced spectroscopy on a sample (15) is shown. Illumination of the sample (15) is provided by an illumination laser (11). A microsphere (1) is attached to the front lens of the objective lens assembly (2) by a column (13) of optically clear material. This allows the illumination radiation to be focused on the sample (15). Scattered radiation from the illuminated area is collected via the microsphere (1) and the objective lens assembly (2). The scattered radiation passes through the objective lens assembly (2), an optional adjustable pinhole (3) and dichroic mirror (4) to a notch filter (8). The filtered scattered radiation is incident upon an optical grating (6), and subsequently a detector. The provision of the microsphere (1) on a column (13) of optically clear material ensures that it can be retained in a desired alignment and at a desired separation from the front lens of the objective lens assembly (2).
claims:
Claims 1. An apparatus for microsphere-enhanced spectroscopy, the apparatus comprising: an illumination source for illuminating a sample with radiation; a radiation collection arrangement for collecting radiation scattered by the sample; and a spectral analyser for analysing collected radiation; wherein the radiation collection arrangement comprises a base lens and a microsphere lens mounted in fixed relation to the base lens. 2. An apparatus as claimed in claim 1 wherein the illumination source is operable to illuminate the sample with a range of wavelengths of output radiation. 3. An apparatus as claimed in claim 1 or claim 2 wherein the illumination source is operable to illuminate the sample with a single wavelength of output radiation. 4. An apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein illumination from the illumination source is directed on to the sample through the radiation collection arrangement or through at least part of the radiation collection arrangement. 5. An apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the sample is scanned relative to the illumination source and/or radiation collection arrangement by mounting the sample on a scanning stage or by integrating the radiation collection arrangement into a scanning stage. 6. An apparatus as claimed in claim 5 wherein the scanning stage is operable to vary the separation between the microsphere lens and the sample 7. An apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the radiation collection arrangement comprises a microscope and the base lens is the objective lens of the microscope. 8. An apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein fluid is provided between the microsphere lens and the sample. 9. An apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the microsphere lens comprises a microsphere or a truncated microsphere. 10. An apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the microsphere lens is mounted directly to the base lens. 11. An apparatus as claimed in claim 10 wherein the microsphere lens is mounted directly to the base lens by a column of optically clear material extending from a front surface of the base lens to the microsphere lens. 12. An apparatus as claimed in claim 11 wherein the column extends from the edges of the base lens to the edges of the microsphere lens. 13. An apparatus as claimed in claim 11 or claim 12 wherein the column is a tapered column. 14. An apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 9 wherein the microsphere lens is provided on an attachment for mounting to the base lens or a housing for the base lens. 15. An apparatus as claimed in claim 14 wherein the microsphere lens is provided on an attachment comprising: a cap locatable in relation to an outer housing of the base lens; a support sheet affixed to the cap; an adhesive layer provided on 

 the support sheet; and a microsphere lens affixed to the support sheet by the adhesive layer, the microsphere lens aligned to the optical axis of the base lens. 16. An apparatus as claimed in claim 15 wherein the cap is releasably attachable to the housing of the objective lens. 17. An apparatus as claimed in claim 15 or claim 16 wherein the adhesive layer comprises an optical adhesive layer or an optically clear adhesive tape. 18. An apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 14 to 17 wherein the attachment comprises a surface coating layer applied over the adhesive layer and the microsphere lens. 19. An apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 18 for use in Raman, IR or UV spectroscopy. 20. A method of microsphere-enhanced spectroscopy, the method comprising the steps of: illuminating a sample with radiation; collecting radiation scattered by the sample for analysis; wherein the radiation is collect using an arrangement comprising a base lens and a microsphere lens mounted in fixed relation to the base lens. 21. A method as claimed in claim 20 wherein the method is carried out with a fixed separation between the sample and the microsphere lens. 22. A method as claimed in claim 20 wherein the method is carried out with a variable separation between the sample and the microsphere lens. 23. A method as claimed in any one of claims 20 to 22 wherein a range of wavelengths of illumination radiation are used. 24. A method as claimed in any one of claims 20 to 23 wherein monochromatic illumination radiation is used. 25. A method as claimed in any one of claims 20 to 24 wherein the method includes the steps of scanning the sample relative to the illumination source and/or radiation collection arrangement. 26. A method as claimed in claim 25 wherein the method includes the step of processing collected radiation to generate an image of the sample. 27. A method as claimed in any one of claims 20 to 26 wherein fluid is provided between the microsphere lens and the sample.
